title: Admin vs Standard Commands in fabio
summary: fabio has both workspace-scoped commands (for regular users) and admin commands (for Fabric administrators). Use admin commands only when explicitly needed.

command_scopes.workspace_scoped: Most commands operate within a workspace you have access to (Member/Contributor/Admin role). Example: fabio lakehouse list --workspace $WS
command_scopes.tenant_scoped: Some commands operate at tenant level without a workspace: connection, capacity, deployment-pipeline, domain. These use your Fabric permissions.
command_scopes.admin_scoped: The 'fabio admin' command group (49 subcommands) requires Fabric Administrator role. These operate across ALL workspaces and resources in the tenant.

when_to_use_admin: Listing ALL workspaces across the tenant (not just ones you're a member of)
Auditing: listing users, sharing links, external data shares
Granting temporary admin access to workspaces you don't own
when_NOT_to_use_admin: Listing/managing items in YOUR workspaces — use 'fabio item list'
Reading data or definitions — use standard item commands
Managing workspace roles — use 'fabio workspace add-role'

standard_alternatives.admin list-workspaces: fabio workspace list (shows workspaces you have access to)
standard_alternatives.admin list-items: fabio item list --workspace $WS (items in a specific workspace)

error_handling.insufficient_scopes: Admin commands fail with AUTH_REQUIRED if you lack Fabric Administrator role. This is NOT fixable by re-authenticating — you need the admin role assigned in your tenant.
error_handling.fallback_pattern: If admin fails, try the workspace-scoped equivalent. Most read operations have standard alternatives.

what_agents_should_do: Default to workspace-scoped commands — they work for all users
Only use 'fabio admin ...' when the task EXPLICITLY requires tenant-wide access
If admin commands fail with AUTH_REQUIRED, suggest workspace-scoped alternatives
Never assume the user is a Fabric Administrator
